**Chart 4: Part-Of-Speech Prediction**
- Language-only's position varies across the two data scales

. Looking at the legend more carefully—DINO-ViT-Trainable, DINO-Res50, DINOv2-ViT, DINO-ViT, Language, Random-ViT, Random-ViT-Trainable—I can see that the black line rising dramatically from low to high performance is likely the Language-only model, suggesting it performs much better at larger data scales for this task. Looking at the black line in the Part-Of-Speech chart, Language-only starts at the bottom (around 0.64 at 4.3K tokens) but climbs dramatically to the top (around 0.88 at 640K tokens) — a jump from last place to first place, which is the most dramatic shift across all the tasks. content.text: Looking at chart 4 (Part-Of-Speech Prediction) more closely:

In the Part-Of-Speech Prediction chart, the black line (Language-only, marked with black dots) starts at the **bottom** (lowest rank, ~0.64) at 4.3K, and rises dramatically to reach the **top** (~0.88) at 640K — going from last place to first place.

In all other charts (Word Relatedness, Semantic-feature Prediction, Lexical-Relation Prediction), Language-only either stays in the middle or moves up slightly, but doesn't change ranking as dramatically.

<answer>
Part-Of-Speech Prediction. In this task, Language-only (the black line with filled dots) starts at 4.3K at the very bottom rank (lowest accuracy, ~0.64) among all models, and by 640K it rises to the top rank (highest accuracy, ~0.88), surpassing all other models. This represents the most dramatic improvement in relative ranking — from last place to first place.
</answer>
